properties.remark: A SPOTTER 2 MILES NORTH OF WISE RIVER REPORTED SEVERAL 8-10 INCH TREES BLOWN DOWN BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
WIND GUST TO 67 MPH RECORDED AT THE SALMON RAWS.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT A ROOF BLEW OFF OF A TRAILER HOUSE AND SEVERAL LARGE TREE BRANCHES WERE DOWN ON HIGHWAY 93.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED MARBLE, NICKEL, AND QUARTER SIZE HAIL AND WINDS UP TO 45 MPH. DAMAGE TO CAMPER WITH HAIL ACCUMULATED ON THE GROUND.
APPROX. 30 TREES BLOWN DOWN BLOCKING HWY 83. REPORT RELAYED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT. TIME WAS ESTIMATED.
APPROXIMATELY 30 TREES BLOWN DOWN BLOCKING HIGHWAY 83. REPORT RELAYED FROM LAW ENFORCEMENT. TIME WAS ESTIMATED.
PUBLIC REPORTED WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE NEAR LION CREEK NORTHEAST OF CONDON. MANY SPRUCE TREES 1.5 FEET IN DIAMETER BROKE 2 FEET ABOVE THE GROUND. FUR TREES UPROOTED AND LANDED OVER THE CREEK.
STATE EMPLOYEE ESTIMATED TENNIS BALL SIZE HAIL CAUSING EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO HIS VEHICLE. SNOW PLOWS WERE CALLED TO CLEAR ROADWAYS.
LARGE HAIL REPORTED ALONG I-90.
MINOR DEBRIS FLOWS WERE REPORTED ALONG US HIGHWAY 93 JUST SOUTH OF NORTH FORK.

MUD AND DEBRIS FLOWS REPORTED ALONG THE MIDDLE FORK OF THE SALMON RIVER AT MILE MARKERS 13, 15 AND 58. AT MILE MARKER 15 A SIGNIFICANT LOG JAM OCCURRED WHICH BACKED UP THE RIVER AND FLOODED GREYHOUND CAMP.
LOCAL MEDIA RECEIVED REPORTS OF MINOR DEBRIS FLOWS ALONG THE SHOUP ROAD FROM BRIEF HEAVY RAINFALL THAT FELL ON THE UNSTABLE SLOPES.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED HAIL THAT WAS BETWEEN QUARTER AND HALF DOLLAR SIZED ALONG US HIGHWAY 93 NEAR ELK BEND.
SEVERAL DEBRIS FLOWS REPORTED ALONG US HWY 93 NEAR ELK BEND DUE TO .5 INCHES RAIN IN 30 MINUTES.
RAWS STATION IN SALMON REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 66 MPH AT 305 PM.
A 10 MILE STRETCH OF HIGHWAY 93 IS CLOSED BETWEEN SALMON AND CHALLIS DUE TO MUD/ROCK SLIDES.
SEVERAL DEBRIS FLOWS REPORTED ALONG US HWY 93 NEAR ELK BEND DUE TO .5 INCHES RAIN IN 30 MINUTES.
15 TO 20 MINOR MUDSLIDES AND 3 TO 4 MAJOR MUDSLIDES OCCURED 20 MILES NORTH OF ELLIS IN LEMHI COUNTY.
15 TO 20 MINOR MUDSLIDES AND 3 TO 4 MAJOR MUDSLIDES OCCURED 20 MILES NORTH OF ELLIS IN LEMHI COUNTY.
NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES NORTH AND SOUTH ALONG HIGHWAY 93 NEAR THE LEADORE AREA.
NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES NORTH AND SOUTH ALONG HIGHWAY 28 NEAR THE LEADORE AREA.
A 10 MILE STRETCH OF HIGHWAY 93 IS CLOSED BETWEEN SALMON AND CHALLIS DUE TO MUD/ROCK SLIDES.
HALF DOLLAR TO PING PONG BALL SIZED HAIL COVERED GROUND
4 INCHES QUARTER SIZE HAIL.
HAIL ACCUMULATION 4 INCHES ON GROUND AT HOLLAND LAKE LODGE.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED AN ESTIMATED WIND GUST TO 60 MPH.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES DOWN. POWER IS OUT TO TOWN. NUMEROUS FIRE STARTS WERE ALSO REPORTED.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED NUMEROUS TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES DOWN. POWER IS OUT TO TOWN. NUMEROUS FIRE STARTS WERE ALSO REPORTED.
A METAL ROOF ON ONE OF THE LARGER BUILDINGS OF THE ROCK CREEK LODGE WAS BLOWN OFF FROM SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
EMPLOYEE REPORTED 53 MPH GUSTS, 2 TREE TOPS BLOWN OFF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A NEIGHBORS ROOF.
SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW DOWN A NUMBER OF COTTONWOOD TREES ALONG I-90 IN NORTHERN GRANITE COUNTY. ONE OF THE COTTONWOOD TREES FELL ONTO THE EAST BOUND LANE OF THE HIGHWAY.
EMPLOYEE REPORTED 53 MPH GUSTS, 2 TREE TOPS BLOWN OFF AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A NEIGHBORS ROOF.
EMPLOYEE REPORTED 1-1.25 INCH HAIL ON INTERSTATE 90 BETWEEN CLINTON AND ROCK CREEK.
EMPLOYEE REPORTED 1-1.25 INCH HAIL ON INTERSTATE 90 BETWEEN CLINTON AND ROCK CREEK.
